fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Nevada Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Nevada - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity nevada, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to nevada, speech-language pathologist compact nevada, nevada speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners for Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
board.parent_dept: Nevada Board of Examiners for Audiology and SLP
board.mailing_address: 5011 Meadowood Mall Way, Suite 300, Reno, NV 89502
board.physical_address: 5011 Meadowood Mall Way, Suite 300, Reno, NV 89502

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Nevada.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Meet the Education Requirements
steps.description: Complete a master’s degree or doctoral degree in speech-language pathology from a program accredited by the Council on Academic Accreditation (CAA) in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Professional Experience
steps.description: Complete a supervised professional experience consisting of at least 1260 hours, with at least 36 weeks of full-time experience.

steps.title: Pass the National Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology administered by the Educational Testing Service (ETS).

telepractice_text: Nevada permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Nevada issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Nevada is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a Nevada SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: $300 initial. $200 biennial renewal. Nevada has among the highest SLP licensing fees.

faqs.question: Is Nevada part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Nevada allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Nevada have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Nevada. To work in Nevada, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Nevada?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Nevada while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Nevada is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Nevada
hero.dek: Nevada Speech-Language Pathology License requires a master's degree, supervised professional experience, and passing a national exam.
